question: Compare the simplified RCS diagram (Figure 5.1-2) with the detailed schematic flow diagram (Figure 5.1-3). What additional detail does the schematic provide?

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is responsible for removing heat from the reactor core and transferring it to the steam generators using natural circulation, driven by density and elevation differences. The RCS includes key components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated valves, and interfaces with several other systems for safety and operational control, including degasification and emergency core cooling. The system is designed to maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary and facilitate the removal of noncondensible gases during both normal and emergency operations.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: The System Evaluation section describes how the NuScale Power Plant optimizes performance across different power levels by establishing steady-state parameters for both primary and secondary systems, such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressures. These parameters are calculated using thermal hydraulic software and verified by hand, with uncertainties and design margins considered to ensure efficient operation and safety. The design supports natural circulation with low pressure losses, and uncertainties like SG tube plugging do not affect primary coolant flow determination.

nuscale_ch05_0000.title: Preface
nuscale_ch05_0000.summary: The Preface outlines the copyright and usage restrictions for the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application, specifically Chapter Five on the Reactor Coolant System and Connecting Systems. It specifies that only the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) is authorized to use, copy, or disclose the information for regulatory purposes, and any copies must retain the copyright notice. The section also references the table of contents for Chapter Five, which covers the summary description and design basis of the system.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain fuel integrity, minimize solid deposits, and ensure uniform boron concentration under all operating conditions, supporting both performance and safety requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0002.title: Summary Description
nuscale_ch05_0002.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping to transfer heat from the reactor core to the steam generator, ensuring safe and efficient operation. The RCS design supports up to twelve independent modules, maintains proper coolant chemistry, and ensures uniform boron concentration to protect fuel integrity during all operating conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for materials and components used in reactor systems.

nuscale_ch05_0012.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Leakage Detection
nuscale_ch05_0012.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) leakage detection system does not use traditional components with designed leakage rates, making it impractical to distinguish between identified and unidentified leakage inside containment with current instrumentation. All leakage into containment is treated as unidentified until the source is located, and leakage rates are determined by RCS inventory balance and comparison to the containment evacuation system (CES) flow rate, with operational limits specified in plant technical specifications. The detection systems are designed to be reliable, redundant, and sensitive to support leak-before-break analyses.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the NuScale pressurizer, including low alloy steel for the baffle plate with stainless steel and nickel-based cladding for corrosion protection, and specifies the use of corrosion-resistant alloys for heater bundles, cover plates, and bolting. All materials are selected for compatibility with operating environments and comply with relevant BPVC Section II specifications.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer in the NuScale reactor is integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and functions to regulate reactor coolant system (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations, with instrumentation providing real-time pressure and level data to control systems.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the NuScale Power Module’s Reactor Coolant System (RCS), detailing its main components such as the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety and ECCS valves, and associated piping and systems. It highlights that the steam generators are integral to the reactor vessel, with specific boundaries separating the primary and secondary sides, and references other sections for detailed design bases of individual components.

nuscale_ch05_0023.title: Reactor Coolant System High-Point Vents
nuscale_ch05_0023.summary: This section outlines the testing and inspection requirements for the DHRS (Decay Heat Removal System) components in the NuScale reactor, ensuring compliance with ASME codes for Class 2 components. It specifies that DHRS actuation valves are exercised during cold shutdown conditions, and details the procedures for valve testing, position verification, and a one-time in-situ test of the DHRS function during initial startup.

nuscale_ch05_0007.title: Integrity of Reactor Coolant Boundary
nuscale_ch05_0007.summary: This section describes the design features of each NuScale Power Module (NPM) that ensure the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) throughout the plant’s design life, in accordance with regulatory definitions. It details the components and systems included in the RCPB, such as the reactor pressure vessel, containment isolation valves, safety valves, and associated piping, emphasizing their roles in maintaining pressure containment and system safety.

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and its reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components comply with relevant U.S. regulations, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria. It details that these components are designed, fabricated, and tested to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) Section III standards, with specific classifications for different piping systems, and references the applicable ASME BPVC edition and regulatory guides used for certification.

nuscale_ch05_0021.title: Reactor Coolant System Piping
nuscale_ch05_0021.summary: The section discusses the design and degradation assessment of the NuScale reactor coolant system's steam generator (SG) tubing. It notes that the main degradation observed in similar operating reactors is wear from flow-induced vibration or foreign objects, and that NuScale's design—with its helical tubing and novel support structure—does not increase risk compared to existing designs. Prototypic testing and standard SG program guidelines are deemed sufficient, with wear identified as the most likely degradation mechanism and secondary corrosion risks considered low.

edges.description: Section A provides a general description of RCS components and functions, while Section B evaluates system performance by referencing specific parameters and criteria, thus Section B specifies details and operational context for the general system described in Section A.

Section A provides specific operational and component details (e.g., degasification line, reactor vent valves) that elaborate on the general system overview given in Section B, thereby specifying how certain functions described in B are implemented.

Section A discusses the system evaluation and references specific figures (e.g., Figure 5.1-2) for RCS diagrams, while Section B is a preface providing copyright and usage information for the entire document, including the figures mentioned in Section A.

Section A evaluates system performance parameters and references further details about the steam generators, while Section B provides the general design basis and components of the RCS, establishing the context for the more detailed evaluations in Section A.

Section A provides the document's preface and copyright information, while Section B begins the technical summary of the reactor coolant system, with Section A serving as a prerequisite for understanding the context and usage restrictions of Section B's technical content.

Section B provides specific details about RCS features such as the degasification line and reactor vent valves that are only generally described in Section A's summary of the RCS, thus elaborating on the components and functions referenced in Section A.

Section A provides a general overview of the RCS components and function, while Section B evaluates system performance by referencing specific parameters and criteria that build upon the components described in Section A.

Section A provides a summary description of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS), while Section B is a preface that establishes the context and copyright for the detailed technical content, including the diagrams referenced in the analysis question.

Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that underpin the technical and operational details described in Section B's system description of the RCS, such as degasification and pressure boundary integrity.

Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that underpin the technical and quality requirements, while Section B describes the system evaluation process that relies on such standards to establish operational criteria for the NuScale Power Plant.

Section A lists references cited throughout the chapter, while Section B provides the preface and copyright notice for the same chapter, indicating that Section A's references support the technical content introduced in Section B.

Section B provides a general system description of the RCS and its pressure boundary, while Section A specifies detailed operational considerations and limitations for leakage detection within that boundary.

Section B's system evaluation establishes steady state parameters and criteria for optimal RCS performance, which provides context for understanding the operational environment and constraints referenced in Section A's discussion of RCS leakage detection capabilities.
Section A discusses technical details about RCS leakage detection, which are elaborated upon in the broader context of the reactor coolant system introduced in Section B's preface to Chapter Five.

Section A provides specific component and material details of the pressurizer, which are depicted in greater detail in the schematic flow diagram (Figure 5.1-3) compared to the simplified RCS diagram referenced in Section B's general system description.

Section A provides specific component and material details (e.g., pressurizer parts and materials) that are part of the broader system described in Section B's system evaluation, thus specifying the detailed makeup of elements referenced in the overall system performance discussion.
Section A provides specific component and material details referenced in the broader context of the Reactor Coolant System introduced in Section B's chapter preface.

Section B provides a general system-level description of the RCS and its degasification function, while Section A gives specific design details about the pressurizer component that is referenced in Section B's description.

Section B's system evaluation references the reactor coolant system and its components, including the pressurizer described in Section A, and both sections are visually connected through the diagrams (Figures 5.1-2 and 5.1-3) that illustrate system details at different levels of complexity.

Section A provides specific technical details about the pressurizer component, which is one of the elements depicted in the diagrams referenced in the analysis question, while Section B is a general preface that frames the context of the entire document including both diagrams.

Section A provides a general overview of the RCS components and subsystems, while Section B offers specific operational details about certain lines and valves (e.g., degasification line, reactor vent valves) that are part of those components, thus specifying additional functions and connections depicted in the detailed schematic (Figure 5.1-3) compared to the simplified diagram (Figure 5.1-2).
Section B's system evaluation references and builds upon the component and subsystem design details provided in Section A to assess system performance and optimization.
Section A provides specific technical details about the Reactor Coolant System components and subsystems, which are elaborated upon in the diagrams referenced in Section B's preface to Chapter Five.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system's design and function, while Section B lists external standards and guidelines referenced for ensuring the integrity and quality assurance of the reactor coolant boundary described in Section A.

Section A provides a general overview of the RCS design and its components, while Section B offers specific details about the leakage detection approach and limitations for the same system, thus Section B specifies operational and monitoring details that elaborate on Section A's general description.
Section B provides detailed component and material information (e.g., pressurizer internals and materials) that elaborates on the general system description given in Section A.

Section A provides a general overview of the RCS and mentions the integral pressurizer as a component, while Section B gives detailed design information about the pressurizer's structure and function within the RCS.

Section B provides detailed component and subsystem information—such as the inclusion of steam generators, safety valves, ECCS valves, and specific piping—not mentioned in the more general overview of the RCS in Section A, thereby specifying the general description given in Section A.
Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that underpin the technical and regulatory basis for the leakage detection approach described in Section B.
Section B provides detailed component and material information about the pressurizer, which is a specific subsystem depicted more generally in the simplified RCS diagram referenced in Section A.

Section A provides references that establish the technical and regulatory basis for RCS boundary integrity, while Section B describes the design and function of the pressurizer as a component within the RCS, connecting the general regulatory framework to a specific system element.

Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that underpin the design and analysis described in Section B, which details the specific components and subsystems of the Reactor Coolant System.

Section B provides detailed specifications of pressurizer components and their materials, which are referenced in Section A's discussion of the reactor coolant pressure boundary and its leakage detection.
Section B provides specific design details about the pressurizer, which is a component referenced in Section A's discussion of the reactor coolant pressure boundary and its leakage detection.

Section B provides a detailed description of the RCS components and subsystems, which supports Section A's discussion of leakage detection by clarifying what equipment and boundaries are relevant for leakage sources.
Section A provides specific material details and component references for the pressurizer, which are general structural and functional elements described in Section B.

Section A provides specific material details and component descriptions for the pressurizer, which is one of the general components listed in Section B's overall description of the Reactor Coolant System.
Section A provides specific details about the pressurizer's structure and function, which elaborates on the general mention of the pressurizer as a component within the RCS described in Section B.

Section A discusses inspection and classification requirements for DHRS components and actuation valves, while Section B describes the function and configuration of reactor vent valves and degasification lines in the RCS, with both sections connected by their focus on the design, function, and regulatory compliance of RCS components related to venting and pressure boundary integrity.

Section A specifies inspection and classification requirements for DHRS components, which are part of the system described in Section B's evaluation of the DHRS connection and performance within the reactor coolant system.

Section A provides specific inspection and classification details for Reactor Coolant System components referenced in the broader context of the NuScale Standard Plant documentation introduced in Section B.

Section B provides specific system features and operational details (such as degasification lines and vent valves) that support the general design objective of maintaining the reactor coolant pressure boundary integrity described in Section A.

Section B's system evaluation relies on the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary described in Section A, as the performance and safety assessments assume the RCPB is maintained according to regulatory definitions.

Section A states that the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components are designed and constructed to meet ASME Code requirements, while Section B describes specific system features (like degasification lines and vent valves) that are part of the RCS and contribute to maintaining RCPB integrity, thus providing concrete examples of compliance.

Section A establishes that the reactor coolant pressure boundary components comply with regulatory codes, while Section B discusses system evaluation criteria that rely on those components' integrity and performance, thus Section A's compliance is a prerequisite for the evaluations in Section B.

Section B provides a general system-level description of RCS features such as degasification and pressure boundary integrity, while Section A offers detailed design considerations and operational experience for specific RCS components (e.g., SG tubing), thus Section A specifies details for the general concepts introduced in Section B.

Section B provides a high-level system evaluation and references further details about the steam generators in Section 5.4, which are elaborated in Section A, thus Section B references and is supported by the detailed component information in Section A.

Section A provides specific technical details about reactor coolant system piping and steam generator tube design, which are elaborated upon in the broader context introduced by Section B's preface to the chapter.

Section A specifies inspection and classification requirements for DHRS components, which are part of the reactor coolant system, while Section B defines the integrity requirements and scope of the reactor coolant pressure boundary that these components must satisfy.

Section A describes specific inspection and classification requirements for DHRS components in accordance with Section XI and OM Code, while Section B outlines the general regulatory compliance for RCPB components with 10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC Section III, indicating that Section A provides detailed specifications for the general compliance framework described in Section B.

Section A specifies inspection and classification requirements for DHRS components, while Section B discusses observed degradation mechanisms and risk assessments for SG tubing, both addressing aspects of reactor coolant system component integrity and maintenance.

Section A defines the scope and components of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB), while Section B specifies that these components must comply with regulatory codes and standards, directly linking the defined boundary to the required compliance measures.

Section A defines the scope and regulatory requirements for the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B provides specific design details and operational considerations for reactor coolant system piping that must comply with those requirements.

Section A states that the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components, including piping, comply with ASME BPVC Section III requirements, while Section B discusses specific operational experience and design considerations for steam generator (SG) tubing within the RCPB, providing evidence that supports the claim of compliance in Section A.
